title: AI Engineer — Startup
description: You're one of the first AI engineers at a funded startup. You do everything — train models, build APIs, set up infrastructure, talk to customers, and ship features weekly. The pace is intense, the ownership is total, and what you build today is in production tomorrow.

schedule.activity: Check production dashboards — model accuracy, latency, error rates, customer usage
Quick standup with the team (4 engineers) — what shipped, what's blocked
Build a new RAG pipeline for a customer use case — embeddings, chunking, retrieval
Customer call — demo the new feature, gather feedback on accuracy and UX
Lunch at desk — review a competitor's product launch announcement
Fine-tune a model on customer-specific data, evaluate against benchmarks
Write the API endpoint for the new feature, add tests, deploy to staging
Pair with the frontend engineer on the AI response streaming UX
Architecture discussion with CTO — scaling strategy for 10x user growth
Deploy to production, monitor for 30 minutes, then head home

responsibilities: Build end-to-end ML features from data to deployment
Own model performance and reliability in production
Talk directly to customers and translate needs into AI features
Set up and maintain ML infrastructure
Make buy vs build decisions on models and tools
challenges: Wearing every hat — data eng, ML eng, infra, and sometimes PM
Limited compute budget — can't just throw GPUs at problems
No established best practices — you're creating them
Fewer peers to learn from compared to big tech
rewards: Massive ownership — you build it, you ship it, you own it
Direct customer impact — see your AI help real people
Equity upside if the company succeeds
Rapid skill growth from breadth of work
Shape the culture and technical direction
No bureaucracy — decisions happen in minutes, not months
careerPath: Senior AI Engineer / Tech Lead — 3-5 years
Head of AI / VP Engineering — 5-7 years
Or: start your own AI company with the experience
